title: Budget-Aware Neural Retrieval on MS MARCO: Distilled Bi-Encoder + FAISS Quantization
one_liner: Train a production-grade dense retriever on MS MARCO and rigorously quantify the accuracy-latency-memory tradeoff via embedding distillation and FAISS PQ/IVF indexing.
research_question: Can a distilled bi-encoder retriever, combined with aggressive vector compression (dimension reduction + product quantization), match BM25 and approach uncompressed dense retrieval quality on MS MARCO while reducing index size and improving tail latency on commodity GPUs?

dataset.name: MS MARCO Passage Ranking (8.8M passages + training triples + qrels/dev queries)
dataset.license: MS MARCO Dataset License (Microsoft; research use)
dataset.ingestion_notes: Download and extract collection.tsv (8.8M passages). Stream-parse TSV to avoid RAM spikes; build (1) Lucene BM25 index and (2) dense embedding shards (FP16) for FAISS. Use official qrels/dev.small for fast iteration; optionally switch to full dev for final numbers. Ensure enough disk for artifacts: passages + Lucene index + embeddings + FAISS index + checkpoints (plan ~200GB).

method.model: PyTorch bi-encoder dense retriever (BERT-base encoder; mean pooling; InfoNCE with in-batch negatives) distilled from a cross-encoder teacher (MiniLM/DeBERTa-style) using KL on soft scores over mined negatives; retrieval via FAISS (FlatIP baseline, then IVF-OPQ-PQ compressed index).
method.baseline: BM25 (Lucene/pyserini) on MS MARCO passages; report nDCG@10/MRR@10/Recall@{10,100} on dev.small (and full dev for final run).
ablations: Embedding dimension reduction: 768 vs 256 (learned linear projection) before indexing.
FAISS compression sweep: FlatIP vs IVF-PQ (vary nlist, m, nbits) while holding candidate budget fixed.
Hard-negative mining on/off (BM25-mined negatives vs random negatives).
metrics: P50/P95 query latency (ms) for top-k retrieval at fixed k

risks: BM25 index build time can dominate (hours) and requires Java/Lucene tooling; mitigate by running dev.small first and caching the index.
FAISS IVF/PQ hyperparameters can cause unstable recall if mis-tuned; mitigate with a small grid under fixed candidate budgets.
Encoding 8.8M passages is GPU-time heavy; mitigate with sharded encoding, resumable checkpoints, and optional passage subsampling for early experiments.
Disk usage can exceed expectations once indexes/checkpoints accumulate; mitigate with an artifacts/ directory and explicit cleanup targets.
execution_steps: Create repo and env (Python 3.10+), install: torch, transformers, sentencepiece, faiss-gpu, pyserini (or lucene), pyarrow, numpy, tqdm, wandb(or tensorboard).
Run dataset download script to fetch and verify MS MARCO files from the listed URLs; extract collection.tar.gz to data/msmarco/.
Build BM25 index over collection.tsv; run BM25 evaluation on queries.dev.small.tsv + qrels.dev.small.tsv; save metrics JSON.
Train cross-encoder teacher on triples.train.small.tsv (or use a publicly available checkpoint if you choose to include it in-repo via config); evaluate as reranker to sanity-check data pipeline.
Train bi-encoder student with contrastive loss; then enable distillation loss using teacher scores over mined negatives; log training curves and validation nDCG@10.
Encode all passages (sharded) to FP16 embeddings; build FAISS FlatIP index; evaluate retrieval metrics and latency.
Build compressed FAISS IVF-OPQ-PQ indexes across a small sweep; evaluate accuracy vs (index size, latency) and select Pareto-optimal configs.

Run ablations (at least: no-distillation, and one compression setting change) and produce a final report table + plots (accuracy vs size/latency) with seeds and confidence intervals via bootstrap on queries.
